volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Truculentus (Section 5.1.919b - Section 5.1.965)

sections.label: Section 5.1.919b
sections.original: Iam ne abis , postquam aurum habes ?
sections.translation: Are you going now , after you ’ ve got the gold ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.920
sections.original: Condidi intro quod dedisti .
sections.translation: What you ’ ve given me , I ’ ve put away in-doors .

sections.label: Section 5.1.920b
sections.original: Ades , amica , te adloquor .
sections.translation: Come here , sweetheart ; I ’ ve got something to say to you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.921
sections.original: At ego ad te ibam , mea delicia .
sections.translation: Why , I was just coming to you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.921b
sections.original: Hercle vero serio ,
sections.translation: In serious truth , i ’ faith .

sections.label: Section 5.1.922
sections.original: quamquam ego tibi videor stultus , gaudere aliqui me volo ;
sections.translation: Although I seem a simpleton to you , I like myself to have a bit of recreation . For pretty though you are , you are so to your own loss , unless I amuse myself a bit with you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.924
sections.original: Vin te amplectar , savium dem ?
sections.translation: Should you like me to embrace you and give you a kiss ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.924b
sections.original: Quidvis face qui gaudeam .
sections.translation: Do whatever you like , I ’ ll deem it agreable .

sections.label: Section 5.1.925
sections.original: Meosne ante oculos ego illam patiar alios amplexarier ?
sections.translation: What , shall I suffer her to be embracing other men before my eyes ? I ’ faith , ’ twere better that I were dead . Woman , take your hands off of him , unless , perhaps , by this sword of mine , won from the enemy , you wish yourself and him to die .

sections.label: Section 5.1.928
sections.original: Nil halapari satiust , miles , si te amari postulas ;
sections.translation: There ’ s no use in badinage [*] , Captain . If you want yourself to be loved , with gold , Stratophanes , not with iron , may you prevent him from loving me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.930
sections.original: Qui , malum , bella aut faceta es , quaé ames hominem isti modi ?
sections.translation: How , the plague , are you pretty or witty , to be fond of a fellow of that description ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.931
sections.original: Venitne in méntem tibi quod verbum in cavea dixit histrio :
sections.translation: Don ’ t it come to your recollection what an actor once said upon the stage ? All people have an eye to their profit , and are not over delicate .

sections.label: Section 5.1.933
sections.original: Huncine hominem te amplexari tam horridum ac tam squalidum ?
sections.translation: That you could possibly caress this fellow , so dirty and foul !

sections.label: Section 5.1.934
sections.original: Quamquam hic squalet , quam quam hic horret , scitus et bellust mihi .
sections.translation: Although he is dirty , although he is foul , still , he ’ s pretty to me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.935
sections.original: Dedin ego aurum —
sections.translation: Didn ’ t I give you some gold ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.935b
sections.original: Mihi ? dedisti filio cibaria .
sections.translation: To me ? You gave money for the child ’ s food .

sections.label: Section 5.1.936
sections.original: nunc , si hanc tecum esse speras , alia opust auri mina .
sections.translation: Now , if you hope to have her , another mina of gold is requisite .

sections.label: Section 5.1.937
sections.original: Malam rem is et magnam magno opere , serva tibi viaticum .
sections.translation: A sore mishap upon these people , and a weighty one !

sections.label: Section 5.1.938
sections.original: Quid isti debes ?
sections.translation: What does she owe you ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.938b
sections.original: Tria .
sections.translation: Three things .

sections.label: Section 5.1.938c
sections.original: Quae tria nam ?
sections.translation: What , pray ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.938d
sections.original: Vnguenta , noctem , savium .
sections.translation: Perfumes , her favours , and kisses .

sections.label: Section 5.1.939
sections.original: Par pari respondet . verum nunc saltem , et si istunc amas ,
sections.translation: He answers him like for like . ( To STRATOPHANES .) But now , at all events , if you do love me ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.940
sections.original: dan tu mihi de tuis deliciis † sum quicquid pauxillulum ?
sections.translation: do you give me some little trifle from your most abundant treasures .

sections.label: Section 5.1.941
sections.original: Quid id , amabo , est quod dem ? dic tum super feri †
sections.translation: Do say , there ’ s a dear , what it is that I ’ m to give you ; only say . If I have it left , you shall have it .

sections.label: Section 5.1.942
sections.original: † capas dicit auaui consultam istuc mihi homo †
sections.translation: Mere kickshaws [*] you ’ re talking about . Be off , be off .

sections.label: Section 5.1.943
sections.original: cave faxis volnus tibi iam cui sunt dentes ferrei .
sections.translation: I ’ ve considered this over with myself . [*] My good sir , take you care , will you , that she don ’ t inflict a wound upon you , whose teeth are made of iron .

sections.label: Section 5.1.944
sections.original: Volgo ad se omnis intromittit .
sections.translation: She ’ s allowing access to her to all in common .

sections.label: Section 5.1.944b
sections.original: Abstine istac tu manum .
sections.translation: You take your hand off of her .

sections.label: Section 5.1.945
sections.original: Iam hercle cum magno malo tu vapula vir strenuos .
sections.translation: Then , by my troth , do you take that , with a hearty punch , warlike man !

sections.label: Section 5.1.946
sections.original: Dedi ego huic aurum .
sections.translation: I ’ ve given her gold .

sections.label: Section 5.1.946b
sections.original: Át ego argentum .
sections.translation: And I , silver .

sections.label: Section 5.1.946c
sections.original: † eat apale puram .
sections.translation: And I a mantle and a purple garment .

sections.label: Section 5.1.947
sections.original: At ego oves et lanam , et alia multa quae poscet dabo .
sections.translation: And I , sheep and wool ; and many other things that she shall ask for I ’ ll give . ’ Twere better for you to contest it with me with minae than with menaces [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.1.949
sections.original: Lepidu'slepidus es ecastor mortalis , mi Strabax , perge obsecro .
sections.translation: Upon my faith you are a funny mortal , my Strabax . Prithee , do proceed —

sections.label: Section 5.1.950
sections.original: Stultus atque insanus damnis certant : nos salvae sumus .
sections.translation: ( Aside .) A fool and a madman are contending for their ruin ; I ’ m all right .

sections.label: Section 5.1.951
sections.original: Age prior prompta aliquid .
sections.translation: Come , younker , do you offer something first .

sections.label: Section 5.1.951b
sections.original: Immo tu prior perde et peri .
sections.translation: Why no ; do you squander first , and come to ruin .

sections.label: Section 5.1.952
sections.original: Em tibi talentum argenti . † Philippices est , tene tibi .
sections.translation: Well , here ’ s a talent of silver for you . It ’ s in Philippean coins . Take it for yourself .

sections.label: Section 5.1.953
sections.original: Tanto melior , noster esto — sed de vostro vivito .
sections.translation: So much the better . Be one of our family , but live at your own expense .

sections.label: Section 5.1.954
sections.original: Vbi est quod tu das ? solve zonam , provocator . quid times ?
sections.translation: Where is that which you are going to give ? Open your purse-strings [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.1.955
sections.original: Tu peregrinu'speregrinus es , hic ego habito : non cum zona ego ambulo :
sections.translation: You are from abroad [*] . I live here ( points to his FATHER ’ S house ) . I am afraid .

sections.label: Section 5.1.956
sections.original: pecua ad hanc collo in crumina ego obligata defero .
sections.translation: I ’ m bringing her some sheep fastened in a purse to my neck .

sections.label: Section 5.1.957
sections.original: quid dedi ! ut discinxi hominem .
sections.translation: Because I gave that , how I did flounder the fellow [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.1.957b
sections.original: Immo ego vero , qui dedi .
sections.translation: Why no , indeed , it ’ s I , who am going to give .

sections.label: Section 5.1.958
sections.original: I intro , amabo , i , tú eris mecum ; tum tu eris mecum quidem .
sections.translation: Come in-doors now , prithee , and ( to STRABAX ) do you then stay with me here .

sections.label: Section 5.1.959
sections.original: Quid tu ? quid ais ? cum hocin eris ? ego ero posterior , qui dedi ?
sections.translation: What do you say ? What , with this fellow ? Am I to be postponed ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.960
sections.original: Tu dedisti iam , hic daturust : ictuc habeo , hoc expeto .
sections.translation: You have given ; ( pointing to STRABAX ) he ’ s going to give just now ; the one I ’ ve got , the other I expect . But each of the two shall be indulged to his heart ’ s content .

sections.label: Section 5.1.962
sections.original: Fiat . ut rem gnatam video , hoc accipiundumst quod datur .
sections.translation: So be it . As I see the matter stands , that must be taken that ’ s offered .

sections.label: Section 5.1.963
sections.original: Meum quidem te lectum certe óccupare non sinam .
sections.translation: Indeed , I shall assuredly not be letting you take possession of my couch .

sections.label: Section 5.1.964
sections.original: Lepide ecastor aucupavi atque ex mea sententia ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ ve cleverly netted them , and quite to my satisfaction .

sections.label: Section 5.1.965
sections.original: meamque ut rem video bene gestam , vostram rursum bene geram :
sections.translation: ( To the AUDIENCE .) And as I see my affairs successfully managed , yours likewise [*] would I successfully manage . I ’ ll caress you in reality . If you are disposed to be doing anything , take care , will you , and let me know at once . For the sake of Venus , applaud ; this Play is in her honor . Spectators , kindly farewell ; grant applause , and then rise up [*] .
